content.problemStatement:
You are given three positive integers N,xN, xN,x, and yyy.

Count the number of arrays PPP of length NNN such that:


P1=PN=1P_1 = P_N = 1P1‚Äã=PN‚Äã=1
There are exactly xxx indices iii such that Pi=1P_i=1Pi‚Äã=1
There are exactly yyy indices iii such that Pi=2P_i=2Pi‚Äã=2
There are exactly N‚àíx‚àíyN-x-yN‚àíx‚àíy indices iii such that Pi=3P_i=3Pi‚Äã=3
Pi‚âÝPi+1P_i \neq P_{i+1}Pi‚ÄãÓÄÝ=Pi+1‚Äã for each 1‚â§i<N1 \leq i \lt N1‚â§i<N
Since the answer can be large, output it modulo 998244353998244353998244353.


Input Format

The first and only line of input will contain three space-separated integers N,xN, xN,x and yyy.


Output Format
Output a single integer: the number of arrays obtained, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
